mapping.a_element: quasinormal modes lying below the real axis (complex frequencies with a decay rate)
mapping.b_element: exponential decay of sentiment shocks / short-memory relaxation of the sentiment process

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is semiclassical resonance-counting: a Weyl-law asymptotic ($C\lambda^3$) for the number of quasinormal modes of the Regge-Wheeler operator, built from a pseudodifferential calculus near threshold energies plus complex scaling and resolvent estimates. Paper B's machinery is empirical time-series persistence analysis of a scalar sentiment index (residence times, bimodality, long- vs short-memory decay, an endogenous-memory model). Both incidentally involve exponential decay of perturbations, but that overlap is generic; A counts spectral resonances of a differential operator while B measures autocorrelation/persistence of a stochastic signal, so no role-for-role mapping of the governing processes holds.
